summary.en: On-demand ride-hailing platform founded 2009 (initially as UberCab black-car service); UberX peer-to-peer launch 2012 made it the textbook two-sided platform: riders pay an app-set fare via card-on-file, drivers receive ~70-85% (Uber takes 15-30% commission). Surge / dynamic pricing is the canonical 'yield management' mechanic that matches supply to demand minute-by-minute. Blitzscaled globally 2013-2018, IPO 2019 at ~$75B. Sub-businesses (Uber Eats 2014, Uber Freight 2017, Uber Health) reuse the same dispatch + payments + driver-network infrastructure for adjacent two-sided markets. Notable mechanic: 'negative cash conversion cycle' — Uber collects from riders instantly but pays drivers on a delay, so growing volume actually generates float. The case shows the asymmetric BMC pair — riders (subsidy-side scale) + drivers (revenue-extraction side) — that codifies a pure two-sided platform with surge pricing and network effects.
summary.zh: 按需打车平台,2009 年创立(最初是 UberCab 高端黑车服务);2012 年 UberX 同城拼车上线后,成为教科书级的双边平台:乘客通过 App 自动定价并用绑卡支付,司机拿到约 70-85%(Uber 抽取 15-30% 佣金)。Surge 动态定价是经典的「Yield Management」机制 —— 按分钟匹配供需。2013-2018 年全球闪电扩张,2019 年以约 750 亿美元上市。子业务(2014 年 Uber Eats、2017 年 Uber Freight、Uber Health)复用同一套调度 + 支付 + 司机网络基础设施去做相邻的双边市场。一个值得关注的机制:**负现金转换周期** —— Uber 从乘客收款是即时的,给司机付款是延后的,所以业务量增长实际上会生成 Float。本案例呈现了那对不对称 BMC —— 乘客侧(规模补贴侧)+ 司机侧(收入抽取侧) —— 把带 Surge 定价和网络效应的纯双边平台讲清楚。

sources.label: Brad Stone · The Upstarts: How Uber, Airbnb, and the Killer Companies of the New Silicon Valley Are Changing the World · Little, Brown · 2017
Reid Hoffman & Chris Yeh · Blitzscaling · Currency · 2018 (Uber as worked example)
Osterwalder & Pigneur · Business Model Generation · Wiley · 2010 (Pattern No. 3, multi-sided-platforms pp. 76-81)

headline.en: A dense platform case: read the mechanism before the growth story
headline.zh: 一个高密度平台案例：先读机制，再读增长故事

whyOpen.en: Uber is useful because it compresses two-sided markets, network effects, cold start, dynamic pricing, subsidies, multi-homing, and cash-flow timing into one case.
whyOpen.zh: Uber 值得学，是因为它把双边市场、网络效应、冷启动、动态定价、补贴、多归属和现金流时点压缩在一个案例里。

audience.en: Readers learning platform strategy or trying to explain why marketplace growth is not just user acquisition.
audience.zh: 适合学习平台战略，或想解释为什么市场平台增长不只是获客的读者。

commonMisreads.en: Uber is not simply an app plus drivers; the hard part is balancing liquidity, incentives, trust, regulation, and take rate across two sides.
commonMisreads.zh: Uber 不是“一个 App 加司机”；难点在于同时平衡流动性、激励、信任、监管和抽成率。

practicePrompts.en: If you removed surge pricing, which side would leave first and why?
practicePrompts.zh: 如果去掉动态定价，哪一边会先离开？为什么？

practicePrompts.en: Which side should be subsidized in your own marketplace, and what behavior proves the subsidy is working?
practicePrompts.zh: 你自己的市场平台应该补贴哪一边？什么行为证明补贴有效？
